name: Blue Mussel Velouté with Saffron Foam & Compressed Sea Beans
description: A world-class main course inspired by Chef Yannick Alléno’s modern extraction philosophy. This dish features ultra-clean mussel essence, high-stability saffron foam, and vacuum-compressed sea beans for a multi-textural marine experience.
keywords: mussel veloute, Yannick Alleno inspired, saffron foam, modern French, seafood extraction, molecular gastronomy, fine dining mussels

steps.instruction: Sweat shallots in a heavy-bottomed pot without color. Increase heat, add mussels and wine, cover, and steam for exactly 3 minutes.
Strain immediately, reserving all liquor. Deshell mussels and store in a small amount of liquid to maintain moisture.
Pass the liquor through a fine-mesh chinois lined with a coffee filter. Reduce by half on an induction system.
Add heavy cream to the reduction and simmer until the sauce coats a spoon. Hold at exactly 70°C using the Control Freak to maintain emulsion.
For the foam: Combine 200ml reserved liquor, saffron, lecithin, and xanthan gum. Use the Sonicator for 90 seconds to extract color and create a permanent emulsion.
Compress sea beans: Place sea beans and 1 tbsp liquor in a vacuum bag. Use the Chamber Vacuum Sealer on high to infuse flavor and alter texture.
Plate: Arrange 5-7 mussels in the center of a warmed bowl. Pour the velouté around the base.
Garnish with compressed sea beans and finger lime pearls.
Finish by spooning the sonicated saffron foam over the top and adding a fragment of bronze leaf.

winePairings.name: Chablis 1er Cru
winePairings.notes: The minerality and high acidity cut through the rich velouté while echoing the brine of the mussels.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: The effervescence and brioche notes complement the saffron foam and cream base beautifully.
